service: IVX AI Chat Room / IVX Owner AI
bug: Chat does not open on the latest conversation and messages appear to disappear
rootCause: FlatList scrollToEnd was firing too early and was not reliably anchored to the newest message when the chat opened. On initial load the list could be unmeasured, so the scroll-to-bottom call silently failed and the latest messages remained below the fold, making the chat look empty/disappeared. The existing scroll retries were not guarded by a pending flag, so later layout checkpoints could miss the initial anchor.

changes: Added pendingInitialScrollRef to track whether the chat still needs to be anchored to the newest message after first load / conversation switch.

Added scrollToBottomRobust() which retries scrollToEnd with fallback to scrollToIndex(lastItem, viewPosition: 1) across multiple timing checkpoints (80/220/260/500/620/1200ms) to cover Android delayed measurement and dynamic bubble heights.
Updated the initial conversation-load effect to set pendingInitialScrollRef and call scrollToBottomRobust() instead of raw scrollToEnd.
Updated FlatList onLayout and onContentSizeChange to check pendingInitialScrollRef and call scrollToBottomRobust(), clearing the flag once the anchor succeeds.
Changed onContentSizeChange auto-scroll behavior to only scroll when the user is already at the bottom, preventing the list from yanking the viewport while the owner is reading older messages.
Removed unconditional scrollToEnd on every content size change that could fight with user scroll intent.

gitHubCommit.message: Fix IVX chat: open on latest message, robust scroll-to-bottom, prevent disappearing

conclusion: The IVX AI chat room client now has a robust open-on-latest mechanism that mirrors WhatsApp/iMessage behavior: the thread lands on the newest message on first load and conversation switch, with retry guards and layout-aware fallbacks so the latest messages never appear to disappear. The code change is committed to GitHub and the frontend Render deploy is queued with the correct commit.
